guide.title: Guide
guide.subtitle: A brief overview of all Dock Scheduling System features.

intro.title: About the app
intro.desc: Dock Scheduling System is used to plan and manage unloading operations at warehouses. Suppliers book time slots at gates, and warehouse workers approve reservations and manage the unloading process.
intro.roleAdminDesc: Manages the entire system — warehouses, gates, users, clients and suppliers.
intro.roleWorkerDesc: Approves and rejects reservations, manages the unloading process at gates.
intro.roleClientDesc: Views reservations directed to their organization, can request changes.
intro.roleSupplierDesc: Creates reservations, enters vehicle, driver and goods details.

registration.title: Account Registration
registration.desc: New suppliers can register themselves via the public registration form. After registration, the account awaits administrator approval.
registration.point1: On the login page, click the 'Register' link.
registration.point2: Fill in your name, email, password, and a message for the administrator (who you are, which company you belong to).
registration.point3: After registration, you can log in but will see a 'Pending approval' banner and cannot create reservations.
registration.tip: In the message for the administrator, mention which client (recipient) you deliver for — this makes approval and organization assignment faster.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.desc: The calendar displays all reservations on a timeline organized by gates. Each gate is a column, and reservations are colored blocks.
calendar.point1: Select a warehouse from the dropdown at the top to filter gates.
calendar.point2: Click a reservation to view its details.
calendar.point3: Color coding: confirmed reservations vs. pending approval.
calendar.tip: Example: Want to check if Gate A is free on Thursday afternoon? Open the calendar, select the warehouse and look at the Gate A column.
calendar.tipWorker: Click a reservation in the calendar and change its status right away — start or complete unloading.
calendar.tipSupplier: The calendar shows only your reservations. Check whether your preferred time slot at the gate is available.
calendar.tipClient: Track all incoming deliveries from your suppliers in one place using the calendar.

reservations.title: Reservations
reservations.desc: A reservation is a request for a time slot at a specific gate for unloading goods. It includes vehicle, driver and transport unit information.
reservations.step1: Click "New reservation" (available for suppliers and admins).
reservations.step2: Select a gate, client, date and time.
reservations.step3: Fill in vehicle details (type, license plate, driver) and add transport units.
reservations.step4: Submit — the reservation awaits approval by a warehouse worker.
reservations.tip: Example: Supplier PG Logistics wants to deliver 10 pallets to Praha-Západ warehouse at Gate B, Thursday 8:00 AM. They create a reservation, enter the license plate and driver name. The warehouse worker approves it, and when the truck arrives, changes the status to "Unloading started".
reservations.descWorker: As a warehouse worker, you approve incoming reservations and manage the unloading process — from start to close.
reservations.descSupplier: As a supplier, you create reservations for specific gates and times. Fill in vehicle, driver and goods details.
reservations.descClient: As a client, you see an overview of all reservations directed to your organization and can track their status.
reservations.tipWorker: When the truck arrives, open the reservation and change the status to "Unloading started". After completion, click "Unloading completed".
reservations.tipSupplier: Fill in the license plate and driver name as soon as possible — it makes the receiving process smoother.
reservations.tipClient: Track your suppliers' reservation statuses. Enable notifications to stay informed about changes.

reservationDetail.title: Reservation Detail
reservationDetail.desc: Each reservation has its own detail page with complete information — vehicle, driver, transport units, advices and full status history.
reservationDetail.point1: Click a reservation in the calendar or reservation list to open the detail.
reservationDetail.point2: Admin and warehouse workers see buttons to approve, reject and change unloading status.
reservationDetail.point3: Status history shows who changed the status and when.
reservationDetail.tip: From the reservation detail you can also directly edit it — change time, vehicle or transport units.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: On the Settings page you can manage your profile and preferences.
settings.point2: Change your password (enter current and new password).
settings.point3: Enable/disable in-app, browser and email notifications.
settings.tip: Email notifications are intended for admins and warehouse workers — enable them to receive alerts about new reservations even when the browser is closed.

mySuppliers.title: My Suppliers
mySuppliers.desc: If the administrator has enabled supplier management for you, you can create accounts for your suppliers. These accounts are automatically verified and ready to use.
mySuppliers.point1: In the 'My Suppliers' section, you see a list of your supplier organizations and their users.
mySuppliers.point2: Use the 'Add Supplier' button to create a new account — choose an existing supplier or create a new one.
mySuppliers.point3: You can also deactivate accounts of suppliers who no longer work with you.
mySuppliers.tip: Accounts created by the client are immediately active — the supplier can log in and create reservations right away.

recurring.title: Recurring Deliveries
recurring.desc: Recurring deliveries allow you to automatically generate regular reservations without manual entry. Set up a template once and the system creates reservations for the entire period.
recurring.point1: Daily (Mon–Fri), weekly (selected days) or monthly (specific day of the month).
recurring.point2: Set validity from–to or open-ended — reservations are generated on an ongoing basis.
recurring.point3: Generated reservations are automatically confirmed — no manual approval required.
recurring.tip: Example: Supplier Procter & Gamble delivers every Monday and Wednesday at 8:00. Set up a weekly recurring delivery for Mon and Wed — the system automatically creates reservations for the whole year.

warehouses.title: Warehouses
warehouses.desc: Manage warehouse facilities. Each warehouse has a name, address and timezone. A warehouse can be deactivated if temporarily unused.
warehouses.tip: Example: Create a warehouse "Praha-Západ" with address and Europe/Prague timezone.

gates.title: Gates
gates.desc: Gates are loading/unloading docks within a warehouse. Set opening hours for each day of the week — reservations cannot be created outside these hours.
gates.tip: Example: Gate A is open Mon–Fri 6:00–18:00, Sat 6:00–12:00, Sun closed.

gateBlocks.title: Gate Blocks
gateBlocks.desc: Blocks allow you to temporarily close a gate for reservations — for example during maintenance, stock-taking or a public holiday. No new reservations can be created during a blocked period.
gateBlocks.tip: Example: Block Gate B from 10:00 to 14:00 on a maintenance day. Existing approved reservations remain, new ones cannot be added.

clientsSuppliers.title: Clients & Suppliers
clientsSuppliers.desc: Clients are organizations receiving goods, suppliers (carriers) deliver them. The client-supplier link determines which suppliers can create reservations for which client.
clientsSuppliers.tip: Example: Client "Allegro CZ" has linked suppliers "PG Logistika" and "DHL Express".

users.title: Users
users.desc: Manage user accounts. The 'All' tab shows existing users, the 'Pending' tab shows new registrations awaiting your approval. Each registration includes a message from the user.
users.tip: When approving a registration, select the supplier organization the user belongs to. Rejected registrations are deleted and the user receives an email notification.

transportUnits.title: Transport Units
transportUnits.desc: Define types of transported goods (pallets, cartons, etc.). Each unit has a default packaging weight and processing time in minutes.
transportUnits.tip: Example: "EUR Pallet" — packaging weight 25 kg, processing 5 minutes.

auditLog.title: Audit log
auditLog.desc: A complete record of all system changes — who changed what and when. Used for compliance and traceability.
auditLog.tip: Example: Want to find out who rejected reservation #42? Open the audit log and filter by entity "Reservation".
